title: Journey
description: CRM-agnostic journey definition. Maps onto Braze Canvas, Klaviyo Flows, Iterable Journeys, Insider Architect — see docs/crm-export-mapping.md.

channel.description: The single source of truth for the channel vocabulary in this schema — referenced by both constraints.allowed_channels and steps[].channel so the two can never drift apart the way a hand-duplicated enum eventually would (the exact bug class already found and fixed in knowledge/channels/*.md's ceilings elsewhere in this repo).

version.description: SemVer of this journey definition; bump on every change so live CRM builds can be diffed against the source

constraints.description: Machine-checkable red lines, embedded from the brand config at generation time so scripts/validate_output.py can enforce them without context

pattern.description: Slug of a file in knowledge/journey-patterns/, e.g. abandoned-cart. JSON Schema can't check filesystem existence — scripts/validate_output.py check_journey cross-references this against the actual knowledge/journey-patterns/<pattern>.md files, the same class of check scripts/validate.sh already runs for industries/pattern_priorities.

vertical.description: For multi-vertical brands only: which of the brand config's `verticals` entries this journey belongs to. Absent for single-industry brands. Was previously only carried in the human-facing journey-doc.md/journey-portfolio.md 'Vertical' column with no machine-facing counterpart — a downstream consumer working from JSON alone (lifecycle-export, an eval script) had no reliable way to group or filter journeys by vertical.

depth_class.description: Mirrors templates/journey-doc.md's header field and the vocabulary every knowledge/journey-patterns/*.md Depth scaling table already uses — was displayed to the user in the markdown doc but had no machine-facing counterpart before this field existed.

type.description: Pattern-level trigger_type values 'segment' and 'absence' both instantiate as segment_entry (the segment is what expresses the absence condition)

value.description: Event name, cron/schedule, or segment id

wait.description: Relative wait, e.g. PT1H, P1D, P1W (ISO 8601 duration — the week designator is mutually exclusive with D/T per the standard, hence the separate alternative). Mirrors scripts/validate_output.py's ISO_WAIT regex — kept in both places deliberately: the schema makes the constraint visible to any external tool reading this file directly, the Python check stays the always-on backstop for when the jsonschema library isn't installed (check_journey falls back to a structural check that skips patterns entirely).

branch_condition.description: Condition to enter this step; null for linear steps

copy_ref.description: Block id in the copy output, e.g. step-2

data_gaps.description: Missing events/attributes that would improve this journey
